opportunities.event_name: Federal Reserve FOMC Meeting Minutes Release
opportunities.rationale: First minutes release under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh; markets expect clues on rate-cut path (1 cut in 2026 vs 2 priced in futures). VIX at 17.81 means any hawkish surprise could spike vol. S&P near ATH — outsized reaction risk. Invalidated if minutes confirm unchanged data-dependent stance with no new guidance.

opportunities.company_name: S&P Global US Flash PMIs — May 2026
opportunities.event_name: S&P Global Flash Manufacturing & Services PMI (May 2026)
opportunities.rationale: April ISM Manufacturing held at 52.7 (4th consecutive expansionary month). May Flash PMIs are the first real-time read on whether momentum continues. With S&P above all MAs, a miss below 50 could trigger rotation out of cyclicals. Invalidated if prior PMI trend proves sticky above 52.

opportunities.company_name: US Housing Starts & Building Permits — April 2026
opportunities.event_name: US Housing Starts and Building Permits (April 2026 data)
opportunities.rationale: Housing data with Fed on hold and mortgage rates elevated. Weak print could pressure homebuilders (DHI, LEN) and home improvement retail (HD). HD already reported Q1 cautiously. Invalidated if housing beats consensus — would support further consumer discretionary strength.

opportunities.company_name: US Initial Jobless Claims — Week ending May 17
opportunities.event_name: US Initial Jobless Claims Weekly Report
opportunities.rationale: Weekly jobless claims are the most current labour market signal ahead of June 6 NFP. Elevated claims would validate Fed dovish signals; low claims reinforce no-cut-needed stance. VIX near 18 means claims surprise can move markets. Invalidated if reading remains in normal 210k-240k range with no surprise.

WTI at $102.47 (TwelveData May 19 close). Energy sector +4.52% WTD — the clear weekly leader. Oil above $100 supported by Middle East risk premium, OPEC+ discipline and global demand recovery. 50-day MA likely around $94-96. Invalidated below $94 (structural demand break or OPEC+ supply shock).
opportunities.entry_trigger: Hold above $100 with energy ETF (XLE) above 20d MA

opportunities.rationale: GLD at $411.88 (MarketWatch May 19 close); Gold spot ~$4,501/oz (TradingEconomics May 19, down 1.44% on day). Elevated safe-haven demand despite equity greed. Fed on hold through June FOMC, real rate uncertainty. EUR/USD at 1.1644 supportive for gold. FOMC minutes on May 20 could be a catalyst — dovish = gold up. Invalidated below $398 GLD if hawkish pivot confirmed.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on or after FOMC minutes if dovish; or hold current position above $408 GLD

opportunities.company_name: ECB June Meeting — Rate Path Signal
opportunities.rationale: ECB deposit rate at 2.00% after March 19 cut. June meeting may confirm further easing given subdued EU inflation. EUR/USD at 1.1644 could weaken on dovish ECB signal. EURUSD short could be viable if ECB cuts while Fed stays on hold. Invalidated if ECB signals pause or inflation surprise forces hawkish tilt.

opportunities.event_name: US Non-Farm Payrolls and Unemployment Rate (May 2026)
opportunities.rationale: Next NFP (May data) on June 6. Under new Fed Chair Warsh with 1 cut projected for 2026, a weak jobs report could front-run that cut and spark broad risk rally. With S&P above ATH and VIX at 17.81, outsized reaction likely. LOW conviction — event too far out for precise positioning. Invalidated if macro data between now and June 6 resets expectations.
